query: we have this utils/retry.ts file that does exponential backoff with jitter, and it's like 80 lines. I feel like there's gotta be a package for this. can you check if we're reinventing anything?
I noticed our codebase has a hand-rolled UUID generator in src/common/id.ts using Math.random(). Are there better alternatives we should be using instead of maintaining this ourselves?
do a build vs buy audit on the domains/validation/ folder — I think we wrote a bunch of validation logic that zod or yup already handles
what libraries are we reinventing the wheel on? scan the whole repo and tell me where we should be using existing packages instead of our custom implementations
check if there are dependency opportunities in our codebase — places where we wrote custom code but a well-maintained open source lib would be better
I just inherited this project and there's a LOT of utility functions in helpers/. Can you figure out which ones are redundant because there's already a library that does the same thing?
our team keeps writing custom date formatting functions. is there a library we should standardize on instead of maintaining all these one-off implementations across the codebase?
run a NIH audit on packages/api/ — I suspect we're maintaining code that established npm packages already handle, especially around HTTP retry and input sanitization
are we using any libraries with known security vulnerabilities? can you scan our dependencies for CVEs and outdated packages
review the code quality of the changes in my last 3 commits — check for complexity violations, coupling issues, and architecture problems
find all the dead code and unused exports in src/domains/ and remove them. I want to slim down the codebase
I need to install express-rate-limit and set it up as middleware. can you help me configure it in our existing Express app?
we have a custom logging framework in src/logger.ts. can you help me add structured JSON output to it? I don't want to replace it, just improve the formatting
check our package.json — are any of our dependencies unmaintained or deprecated? I want to make sure we're not relying on abandoned projects
can you explain how our custom retry logic in utils/http.ts works? I need to understand the backoff algorithm before I modify it
write unit tests for the deepMerge function in src/utils/objects.ts — I want full coverage including edge cases with nested arrays and circular references
help me refactor the authentication module — it's gotten too complex and I want to simplify the flow without changing any external dependencies
what npm packages should I use for a new Express project? I need auth, validation, logging, and rate limiting recommendations
are there any libraries we should migrate to? I feel like half our utils/ folder is stuff that lodash or ramda already does, and the other half is date stuff that date-fns handles
should we use a library for our custom argument parser in bin/cli.ts? it's gotten to 200 lines and I'm not sure it's worth maintaining vs using commander or yargs
